sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a male in his midlife, presented to the office with a complaint of shortness of breath.
He reports that he has noticed this symptom for some time, approximately 2 years, and attributes it to his recent move to the city.
He mentions that he has a plot of land in the country where he enjoys hunting and has a refrigerator full of venison.

He describes his shortness of breath as feeling like he catches his breath more easily than he used to and has noticed it in various situations, including at home, when his cat is present, and when outside.
He denies any other symptoms such as rash, nausea, vomiting, or lip/throat swelling.
He reports having a dog that also experiences eye watering, which he attributes to allergies.
He mentions that he has an inhaler that he uses when symptoms occur and that the last time he experienced symptoms, he was given a nebulizer at urgent care.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of occasional heavy drinking and reports experiencing diarrhea after consuming alcohol.
He denies any other medical conditions or allergies.
Review of systems:
The patient reports no other symptoms besides shortness of breath.
Current medications:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler as needed for asthma.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's pulse is within normal limits, and his oxygen saturation is normal.
His physical examination is notable for faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
The chest X-ray and pulmonary function test results were normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma; Asthma; Allergies: unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed Singulair 10mg once daily to help decrease the occurrences of asthma symptoms.
I also plan to proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing to identify potential triggers.
I want to see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in one week for allergy testing.
